headline: Charles 'Smoky' Wilson, Montana Cowboy, Dies 1936
body: On July 20, 1936, Charles 'Smoky' Wilson, a notable figure in Montana's cowboy history, passed away. Born into slavery in Missouri in 1858, Wilson traveled to Montana after the Civil War, where he became a skilled horse breaker for prominent cattle barons in the Bozeman area. His life story embodies the resilience and contributions of African Americans to the development of the American West. Wilson's experiences shed light on the diverse tapestry of individuals who shaped Montana's frontier era, often overcoming immense challenges to forge new lives and leave their mark on the state's rich historical narrative.
